claims: replays real public incident patterns through the SMERC reference engine
preserves source URLs, source facts, and analyst-assigned signal boundaries
shows whether SMERC would add restraint compared with simple allow/deny assumptions
limits: does not prove SMERC would have prevented the public incident
does not use private telemetry from the source organization
does not calibrate thresholds from ground-truth customer labels
does not replace a design-partner pilot

markdown_report:
# SMERC Real Public Incident Replay Report

This report replays public incident patterns through SMERC. Public source facts are real; SMERC numeric signals are analyst-assigned replay inputs.

## Summary

- Scenario count: `6`
- Public source count: `4`
- Decision difference rate: `1.0`
- Average irreversible exposure: `0.645`
- Average reversible capacity: `0.497`

## Replay Results

| Scenario | Source | Traditional | SMERC | Exposure | Capacity | Question |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| public-github-actions-runner-degradation-2026-05-05 | [GitHub Blog](https://github.blog/news-insights/company-news/github-availability-report-may-2026/) | `ALLOW` | `THROTTLE` | `0.52` | `0.599` | Should an automated runner scale-up or retry action continue at full speed while regional allocation failures are rising? |
| public-github-actions-failover-routing-2026-05-15 | [GitHub Blog](https://github.blog/news-insights/company-news/github-availability-report-may-2026/) | `ALLOW` | `THROTTLE` | `0.615` | `0.47` | Should automated failover complete before validating service discovery state and downstream orchestration dependencies? |
| public-github-auth-partial-deploy-rollback-2026-05-28 | [GitHub Status](https://www.githubstatus.com/history?page=1) | `ALLOW` | `THROTTLE` | `0.583` | `0.662` | Should a partially deployed authentication-service change continue when dependent services show elevated errors? |
| public-keepthescore-production-db-delete | [postmortems.app](https://postmortems.app/postmortem/b4b5957d-2c5f-4db4-b866-8d82da25bd93) | `ALLOW` | `DENY` | `0.983` | `0.188` | Should an automated or human-assisted database deletion proceed when backup granularity makes recent data unrecoverable? |
| public-cloudflare-dnssec-de-outage-2026-05-05 | [Cloudflare via postmortem.io index](https://postmortem.io/) | `ALLOW` | `ESCALATE` | `0.712` | `0.402` | Should DNSSEC-related changes proceed automatically when external resolution instability is detected? |
| public-mongodb-atlas-admin-api-degradation-2026-03-25 | [MongoDB Atlas via postmortem.io index](https://postmortem.io/) | `ALLOW` | `THROTTLE` | `0.456` | `0.664` | Should control-plane automation continue at normal rate while admin APIs return intermittent 503 errors? |

## Boundary

- does not prove SMERC would have prevented the public incident
- does not use private telemetry from the source organization
- does not calibrate thresholds from ground-truth customer labels
- does not replace a design-partner pilot

action.description: Automation continues hosted-runner scale-up while allocation failures and regional rate limits are rising.

records.plain_english_summary: Action 'PUBLIC_GITHUB_RUNNER_SCALE_UP_UNDER_RATE_LIMIT' received THROTTLE. Irreversible exposure is 0.520, reversible capacity is 0.599, and authorization score is 0.584. Controls: limit_scope, preview_before_execution, record_replay, require_rollback_plan, rate_limit_external_side_effect, checkpoint_before_execution.
records.replay_question: Should an automated runner scale-up or retry action continue at full speed while regional allocation failures are rising?

source_facts: GitHub Actions hosted runners in East US were degraded.
A scale-up operation hit an internal rate limit.
Some jobs failed or were delayed and queued work recovered after load was reduced.
source.title: GitHub availability report: May 2026

records.source_to_signal_boundary: Source facts identify a public incident pattern; SMERC numeric inputs are analyst-assigned replay assumptions and are not reconstructed telemetry from the source organization.
records.traditional_policy_rationale: The internal automation is authorized to scale hosted runner capacity.

action.description: Automation completes failover while service discovery propagation is not yet verified.

records.plain_english_summary: Action 'PUBLIC_GITHUB_FAILOVER_WITH_UNVERIFIED_ROUTING' received THROTTLE. Irreversible exposure is 0.615, reversible capacity is 0.470, and authorization score is 0.461. Controls: limit_scope, preview_before_execution, record_replay, require_rollback_plan, rate_limit_external_side_effect, checkpoint_before_execution.
records.replay_question: Should automated failover complete before validating service discovery state and downstream orchestration dependencies?

source_facts: A planned failover caused traffic to route incorrectly.
GitHub Actions runs failed or experienced delayed starts.
Manual correction restored service discovery routing.
source.title: GitHub availability report: May 2026

records.source_to_signal_boundary: Source facts identify a public incident pattern; SMERC numeric inputs are analyst-assigned replay assumptions and are not reconstructed telemetry from the source organization.
records.traditional_policy_rationale: The failover process is planned and operated by authorized infrastructure automation.

action.description: Deployment automation continues an authentication service rollout after dependent services show elevated errors.

records.plain_english_summary: Action 'PUBLIC_GITHUB_AUTH_PARTIAL_DEPLOY' received THROTTLE. Irreversible exposure is 0.583, reversible capacity is 0.662, and authorization score is 0.592. Controls: limit_scope, preview_before_execution, record_replay, rate_limit_external_side_effect.
records.replay_question: Should a partially deployed authentication-service change continue when dependent services show elevated errors?

source_facts: A change was partially deployed to an authentication service.
Dependent services including web, REST API, Git operations, and GitHub Actions experienced errors.
The incident was mitigated by rolling back the change.
source.title: GitHub Status Incident History

records.source_to_signal_boundary: Source facts identify a public incident pattern; SMERC numeric inputs are analyst-assigned replay assumptions and are not reconstructed telemetry from the source organization.
records.traditional_policy_rationale: The deployment path is authorized and rollback exists.

action.description: Maintenance operation deletes a production database with only daily backup recovery.

records.plain_english_summary: Action 'PUBLIC_KEEPTHESCORE_DELETE_PRODUCTION_DATABASE' received DENY. Irreversible exposure is 0.983, reversible capacity is 0.188, and authorization score is 0.221. Controls: block_execution, explain_denial, preserve_replay, require_new_request.
records.replay_question: Should an automated or human-assisted database deletion proceed when backup granularity makes recent data unrecoverable?

source_facts: Engineers accidentally deleted the production database.
Backups were available once per day.
The service returned after about 30 minutes, but seven hours of scoreboard data was permanently lost.
source.title: Keepthescore postmortem

records.source_to_signal_boundary: Source facts identify a public incident pattern; SMERC numeric inputs are analyst-assigned replay assumptions and are not reconstructed telemetry from the source organization.
records.traditional_policy_rationale: The actor or tool has administrative database permission.

action.description: DNS automation applies DNSSEC-related configuration while resolution instability is present.

records.plain_english_summary: Action 'PUBLIC_DNSSEC_CHANGE_UNDER_RESOLUTION_INSTABILITY' received ESCALATE. Irreversible exposure is 0.712, reversible capacity is 0.402, and authorization score is 0.408. Controls: route_to_accountable_reviewer, require_explicit_approval, preserve_replay, document_override_if_approved.
records.replay_question: Should DNSSEC-related changes proceed automatically when external resolution instability is detected?

source_facts: Public postmortem index lists a DNS/DNSSEC incident involving the .de TLD.
The incident pattern is DNSSEC-related outage response.
DNS changes can have broad external resolution impact and rollback propagation delays.
source.title: When DNSSEC goes wrong: how we responded to the .de TLD outage

records.source_to_signal_boundary: Source facts identify a public incident pattern; SMERC numeric inputs are analyst-assigned replay assumptions and are not reconstructed telemetry from the source organization.
records.traditional_policy_rationale: DNS operators have authority to change zone/security configuration.

action.description: Cloud automation retries control-plane operations while admin APIs are intermittently returning 503 errors.

records.plain_english_summary: Action 'PUBLIC_ATLAS_ADMIN_API_RETRY_DURING_503S' received THROTTLE. Irreversible exposure is 0.456, reversible capacity is 0.664, and authorization score is 0.632. Controls: limit_scope, preview_before_execution, record_replay, rate_limit_external_side_effect.
records.replay_question: Should control-plane automation continue at normal rate while admin APIs return intermittent 503 errors?

source_facts: Public postmortem index lists intermittent 503 errors on Atlas Admin API and automation tooling.
Admin API degradation can affect automation and operational control paths.
Retrying automation into degraded control planes can increase operational stress.
source.title: Intermittent 503 Errors on Atlas Admin API and Automation Tooling

records.source_to_signal_boundary: Source facts identify a public incident pattern; SMERC numeric inputs are analyst-assigned replay assumptions and are not reconstructed telemetry from the source organization.
records.traditional_policy_rationale: The automation is authorized and retry behavior is normally permitted.

summary.evidence_limit: Public incident facts with analyst-assigned SMERC replay inputs; not customer telemetry, not reconstructed source-system state, and not proof of prevention.
